fields.label: Choose cursor style for all players.
Check this box and click the green button to reset the game.

loopRoutine.note: This was added by the automatic file migration because the behavior of MOVE with count=0 changed.

actions_details.detail:
Action Options
Build Railroad Track
Spend exactly $5 laying cubes on the board, the terrain cost is shown in the grey aid near the bottom of the screen. May place different color cubes, but colors must go adjacent to previous cubes of that color. Different hex types can hold varying numbers of cubes, but never more than one of a color. You cannot build across a red line.

BIG BONUS: If a cube is placed on a mine hex, all players with at least one mine claim on the track gain 1vp

Start Railroad (First Player Only)
The first player in a round may take an unused cube color and place one in any starting (purple) city in the east, obeying the hex limit of 3.

Shut Down a Mine
Remove one of your claims from the track, gaining no points. Take a new claim from your area and place it on the bottom of the track, with your token on it.

scoring_details.detail:
Scoring Mines
Once all players have taken a turn, score all mine claims where a cube has been placed on the associated mine. Each claim gives that player points equal to the number on that claim. Double scored points if there are 2 or 3 cubes on a mine.

Click on claims for mines that have at least one cube on them, moving the player marker to the left. This makes it easy to tell which claims are scored.

closing_details.detail:
Close Mines
Remove claims from the track equal to the number of players. Begin at the top, removing only scored (player token on the left) claims. If all scored claims are removed and more must be taken, begin at the top once again and remove unscored claims.

Each time a claim is removed, place the player token from it into the grey Replacement Order Track to the left of the claims, beginning at the top and placing new ones to the bottom.

Once enough claims are removed, new claims are added. Starting with the topmost token on the Replacement Order Track, take a claim from your supply and place it to the bottom of the claims track with your token on it.

endgame_details.detail:
Endgame Details
The game ends at the end of a round (after scoring and closing) if any of the following four conditions are met:
• Any player has 60+ points
• Any player has no claims left in their holdings area
• Any player was unable to use all $5 during a Build Railroad Track action.
• Every mine has at least one cube on it.

The player with the most points wins! Ties are possible.

general_details.detail:
General Help
Modifying Points: Click on the 'edit' button next to your points, input the points change, and click the green button.

Ending Your Turn: Click the 'Current Turn' button to the right of your name once your turn is over, so that the next player may perform their turn.

Moving the Train: Begin a new round by moving the train to the next player down (or wrap to the top). Click and drag the train to the right of the next player's name, this will begin a new round.

info.name: Rocky Rails
info.attribution:
Layout, board, cards, design, coding, and library image by Jonathan Cox (email: Contact@GettingGames.com for inquiries) and released to the Public Domain under CC0.

The red squiggle icon by Jonathan Cox is released to the Public Domain under CC0.

Sound while changing turns is "Wrong Answer" by UNIVERSFIELD and is available under the Pixabay License at  https://pixabay.com/sound-effects/wrong-answer-129254/.
info.description: Lay track through the Rockies, trying to capitalize on your random mining portfolio.
info.helpText:
Players take seats. Press the Setup button to begin.

Detailed gameplay and usability information is found by hovering over the brown info boxes in the top-middle of the screen.
